2023-02-01.title: Seeing a Prize, Russia Inundates a Ukraine City With Troops
2023-02-01.abstract: The battle in the eastern Ukraine city of Bakhmut is growing in importance, as both sides pour forces into the battle.

2023-02-01.title: Pope, in Congo, Urges an End to the Country’s Cycle of Violence
2023-02-01.abstract: Francis began the second day of his visit to Africa with a direct appeal to the warring groups in the Democratic Republic of Congo to put down their weapons and forgive one another.

2023-02-01.title: Asia Is Loosening Rules on Masks. Here’s Why People Still Wear Them.
2023-02-01.abstract: Many other countries dropped pandemic mask requirements months ago. But in places like South Korea, which got rid of its rule this week, masks remain common.

2023-02-01.title: Seeking a Needle in a Haystack, Australians Find Lost Radioactive Device in Six Days
2023-02-01.abstract: The authorities had feared it would take weeks to scour hundreds of miles of an Australian desert for the pea-sized device.

2023-02-01.title: India’s Right Wing Tried to Scuttle a Film. Fans Helped It Break Records.
2023-02-01.abstract: “Pathaan” demonstrated the crosscutting appeal of the Bollywood titan Shah Rukh Khan, who re-emerged on the big screen after a difficult personal period.

2023-02-02.title: U.S. to Boost Military Role in the Philippines in Push to Counter China
2023-02-02.abstract: Washington and Manila announced a plan to give the American military access to four new locations in the Southeast Asian country, a growing strategic partner in the region.

2023-02-02.title: China and the U.S. Are Wooing Indonesia, and Beijing Has the Edge
2023-02-02.abstract: The resource-laden nation of nearly 300 million is a big prize in the strategic battle between the United States and China for influence in Asia.

2023-02-02.title: Australia Won’t Put King Charles on Its 5-Dollar Bill
2023-02-02.abstract: The bill had long featured Queen Elizabeth II, but officials said the bank note would be redesigned to focus on Indigenous history. That has rekindled the debate about republicanism in Australia.

2023-02-02.title: A U.S. Ambassador Finds Himself on Hostile Ground in Hungary
2023-02-02.abstract: David Pressman, a gay human rights lawyer, has been accused by pro-government media in Hungary of undermining traditional values, violating diplomatic conventions and meddling in the judiciary.

2023-02-02.title: A Primer on U.S.-Philippine Military Ties
2023-02-02.abstract: The United States and the Philippines have announced a plan to broaden American military access. The alliance is complicated by colonial history and rising tensions over Taiwan.

2023-02-03.title: As Russia Strikes Ukrainian Civilians, Putin Tells His People the War Is Just
2023-02-03.abstract: Invoking World War II on the 80th anniversary of victory at Stalingrad, Mr. Putin repeated his false justifications for an invasion that has taken a staggering toll.

2023-02-03.title: Court Dismisses Guilty Plea by Australian Tennis Star Nick Kyrgios in Assault Case
2023-02-03.abstract: The guilty plea and dismissal stemmed from a confrontation Mr. Kyrgios had with his partner in 2021 when she tried to prevent him from leaving in a ride-hailing car.

2023-02-03.title: The World’s Newest Country Is Broken and Forgotten. Enter Pope Francis.
2023-02-03.abstract: The soaring hopes at South Sudan’s creation in 2011 have been cruelly dashed. Pope Francis arrived in the country on Friday and immediately offered blunt criticism of its leaders.

2023-02-03.title: A New Approach to Funding for the Arts
2023-02-03.abstract: A new government policy, called Revive, will prioritize Indigenous arts and culture, and also provide funding for commercial arts forms, like popular music.

2023-02-03.title: Corporate Lawyer’s Unlikely Mission: Defend and ‘Humanize’ a Hated Terrorist
2023-02-03.abstract: Stanislas Eskenazi volunteered with Brussels’ legal aid service, typically helping petty criminals. But now he is representing Belgium’s most-wanted man.

2023-02-04.title: E.U. Vows More Help for Ukraine but Tamps Down Membership Talk
2023-02-04.abstract: European Union leaders met in Kyiv with President Volodymyr Zelensky, who said Ukraine would not give up on Bakhmut, the eastern city caught in a fierce battle with Russian forces.

2023-02-04.title: Balloon Incident Highlights Fragile State of U.S.-China Relationship
2023-02-04.abstract: Recent efforts by Beijing to mend ties with Washington were showing progress, but the balloon’s appearance over the United States illustrates lingering mistrust and growing tensions.

2023-02-04.title: After Gutting Youth Services, Can the U.K. Still Cut Youth Crime?
2023-02-04.abstract: Poor neighborhoods that have been hit hardest by austerity have also seen violence among young people surge or remain stubbornly high. Residents say that’s no coincidence.

2023-02-04.title: What’s the Correct Color of Bees? In Austria, It’s a Toxic Topic.
2023-02-04.abstract: In the Austrian state of Carinthia, where the law favors light-colored local bees, those honey producers judged “too dark” risk eradication.

2023-02-04.title: When It Comes to Building Its Own Defense, Europe Has Blinked
2023-02-04.abstract: Despite expectations that Russia’s invasion of Ukraine would force Europe to bolster its military strength, it has instead reinforced dependency on U.S. leadership, intelligence and might.

2023-02-05.title: Pervez Musharraf, Former Military Ruler of Pakistan, Dies at 79
2023-02-05.abstract: Mr. Musharraf took power in a bloodless coup in late 1999 but resigned under threat of impeachment in 2008. He drew fire for his ties to Washington.

2023-02-05.title: How China Dealt a ‘Knockout Blow’ to Its Opponents in Hong Kong
2023-02-05.abstract: Beijing used a national security law to quash dissent. Now another warning is being sent, with the mass trial of what was once Hong Kong’s political opposition.

2023-02-05.title: In West Bank, Settlers Sense Their Moment After Far Right’s Rise
2023-02-05.abstract: After a surge in violence, there are fears of a wider escalation in the occupied West Bank. Israeli settlers see an opportunity, and Palestinians fear what’s next.

2023-02-05.title: China Finds Itself With Limited Options After U.S. Shoots Down Balloon
2023-02-05.abstract: Beijing registered “strong discontent and protest.” But there may be little it can do to retaliate.

2023-02-05.title: In China’s Covid Fog, Deaths of Scholars Offer a Clue
2023-02-05.abstract: The toll of China’s epidemic is unclear. But dozens of obituaries of the country’s top academics show an enormous loss in just a few weeks.

2023-02-06.title: Teenage Girl Dies After Shark Attack in Australian River
2023-02-06.abstract: The girl, 16, may have jumped into the water to swim with a pod of dolphins when she was attacked, the authorities said.

2023-02-06.title: Monday’s earthquake was as powerful as the strongest recorded in Turkey in 1939.
2023-02-06.abstract: Turkey, a hotbed of seismic activity, sits on the Anatolian Plate, which borders two major faults as it grinds northeast against Eurasia.

2023-02-06.title: How strong is a magnitude-7.8 quake?
2023-02-06.abstract: Experts fear that the quake in Turkey on Monday was strong and shallow enough to be lethal on a devastating scale.

2023-02-06.title: The earthquake struck war-scarred northern Syria.
2023-02-06.abstract: After more than a decade of conflict, northern Syria will be ill-equipped to recover from Monday’s earthquake amid a collapsing economy.

2023-02-06.title: Mapping the Damage From the Earthquake in Turkey and Syria
2023-02-06.abstract: See where the earthquake caused major damage across dozens of cities.

2023-02-07.title: How Turkey’s Anatolian Fault System Causes Devastating Earthquakes
2023-02-07.abstract: See the fault zones where more than a century of the deadliest earthquakes have occurred.

2023-02-07.title: Monday’s quake is one of the deadliest this century. The toll is expected to rise.
2023-02-07.abstract: Officials in both Syria and Turkey expect the death toll to rise significantly as rescuers make their way through devastated cities.

2023-02-07.title: Zelensky may visit Brussels, in his second known trip abroad since Russia’s invasion last year.
2023-02-07.abstract: The Ukrainian president’s possible visit to the Belgian capital, where he would likely address the European Parliament, hinges on security arrangements.

2023-02-07.title: The U.S. aid agency deploys two search and rescue teams to Turkey.
2023-02-07.abstract: The two units, based in Virginia and California, are the only such teams in the United States that work internationally. They join the thousands of  rescuers from Syria and Turkey already there.

2023-02-07.title: In an apartment block, one tower collapsed while others around it did not.
2023-02-07.abstract: Rescue workers listened for signs of survivors in the rubble as residents wondered why their homes had not fallen.

2023-02-08.title: Videos Show Desperate Search for Survivors Near Quake’s Epicenter
2023-02-08.abstract: Hatay Province had Turkey’s highest-known death toll from Monday’s earthquake.

2023-02-08.title: A Grieving Turkish Diaspora Rallies Together
2023-02-08.abstract: In Melbourne, Australia, dozens of volunteers in the parking lot behind a halal butcher shop packed three shipping containers with cardboard boxes full of new tents, blankets and sleeping bags.

2023-02-08.title: Zelensky thanks Britain during his second overseas trip since Russia’s invasion.
2023-02-08.abstract: LONDON — President Volodymyr Zelensky of Ukraine made a surprise visit to Britain on Wednesday morning, welcomed with a hug at Stansted Airport by Prime Minister Rishi Sunak, who announced plans to expand British military support for Ukraine to include training its pilots in NATO fighter jets.

2023-02-08.title: Syrian Refugees Who Died in Earthquake Are Returned Home
2023-02-08.abstract: Millions fled the war in Syria for the safety of neighboring Turkey. “We want them to be buried among their family,” said a man waiting for the body of a young relative coming from Turkey.

2023-02-08.title: In Rural Ukraine, Medicine and Hope Roll In on a Truck
2023-02-08.abstract: A mobile clinic is trying to restore medical services to villages once occupied by Russian forces as fighting rages nearby. “They’ll never beat our people,” a specialist with the team said.

2023-02-09.title: Zelensky Brings a Shopping List to Britain, With Fighter Planes at the Top
2023-02-09.abstract: In a surprise visit that started a two-day blitz of Western allies, the Ukrainian president implored Britain to provide planes he said would represent “wings of freedom.’’

2023-02-09.title: Millions Suffer Cold and Grief After Catastrophic Quake, Awaiting Help
2023-02-09.abstract: With thousands dead and many more left homeless in Turkey and Syria, people struggling to unearth victims, bury the dead and provide for millions of survivors are pleading for more aid.

2023-02-09.title: In Syria, a Hospital ‘Filled With Tragedy’
2023-02-09.abstract: A subterranean hospital known as the Cave and long the destination for war wounded is now filled with casualties of the earthquake — both alive and dead.

2023-02-09.title: Zelensky presses Ukraine’s case in Brussels, the third stop in a brief but packed European tour.
2023-02-09.abstract: BRUSSELS — President Volodymyr Zelensky of Ukraine was meeting the European Union’s 27 heads of government in Brussels on Thursday, capping a two-day tour of key European allies as he framed Russia’s invasion as an existential struggle for the continent.

2023-02-09.title: Rebuilding Quake-Stricken Cities in Turkey and Syria May Take Decade
2023-02-09.abstract: While intact buildings might look fine, their structural integrity might have been damaged. Some of these buildings will have to be torn down. This process alone could take years.

2023-02-10.title: In Speech to E.U. Leaders, Zelensky Links Europe to Ukraine’s Fate
2023-02-10.abstract: President Volodymyr Zelensky, in only his second trip outside Ukraine since the conflict began, told European lawmakers that his country’s war against Russia was a defense of all Europe.

2023-02-10.title: ‘This Is Our Fate’: A Syrian Family Is Displaced in Southern Turkey
2023-02-10.abstract: Syrian refugees displaced by the earthquake in southern Turkey say they need water and more tents to keep their children warm.

2023-02-10.title: With So Many Dead in Turkey, Families Forced to Rush Funeral Rites
2023-02-10.abstract: The earthquake that hit in southern Turkey has killed more than 17,500 people there, overwhelming the traditional funeral process and accelerating how families say goodbye.

2023-02-10.title: ‘They’re Hunting Me.’ Life as a Ukrainian Mayor on the Front Line
2023-02-10.abstract: Being the leader of Kherson may feel more like a curse than an honor. But one woman isn’t giving up, even though the Russians are sitting just across the river and shelling her city nearly every hour.

2023-02-10.abstract: Only a trickle of humanitarian aid has reached the devastated parts of Syria because years of war have carved the country into zones of control that impede deliveries.

2023-02-11.title: Russia Fires Major Missile Barrage at Ukraine as Combat Intensifies
2023-02-11.abstract: The first major aerial bombardment in weeks targeted cities across Ukraine, as President Volodymyr Zelensky returned home from a trip across Europe to press for more arms.

2023-02-11.title: Toronto Mayor Resigns After Admitting to Relationship With Employee
2023-02-11.abstract: The mayor, John Tory, who had led the city since 2014, apologized for what he called a serious error in judgment.

2023-02-11.title: Wagner Founder Has Putin’s Support, but the Kremlin’s Side-Eye
2023-02-11.abstract: Yevgeny V. Prigozhin, the once secretive tycoon, is confounding Moscow’s Kremlin-allied elite by starting to dabble in politics alongside waging war in Ukraine.

2023-02-11.title: Anger Over Quake Response Challenges Erdogan Ahead of Election
2023-02-11.abstract: A furor is building among some survivors over the government’s handling of the crisis. “I have been voting for this government for 20 years,” said one. “I will never forgive them.”

2023-02-11.title: Ukraine Rushes to Repair After Wave of Russian Strikes
2023-02-11.abstract: Russia hit several regions with Iranian-made drones overnight, capping a day in which its forces unleashed a major barrage of missiles on critical sites around Ukraine.

2023-02-12.title: The Fight Over Fox Hunting: A Cold War on England’s Muddy Fields
2023-02-12.abstract: Some groups still ride out even though the hunting of foxes using dogs was banned in 2004. They are confronted by anti-hunt activists in a very British clash of class, tradition and town versus country.

2023-02-12.title: A Yale Professor Suggested Mass Suicide for Old People in Japan. What Did He Mean?
2023-02-12.abstract: Yusuke Narita says he is mainly addressing a growing effort to revamp Japan’s age-based hierarchies. Still, he has pushed the country’s hottest button.

2023-02-12.title: They Are Russians Fighting Against Their Homeland. Here’s Why.
2023-02-12.abstract: In the Free Russia Legion, soldiers repelled by Vladimir Putin’s invasion have taken arms against their home country, engaged in some of the most heated fighting in the war.

2023-02-12.title: Russia’s Wagner Fighters Claim Advance Near Bakhmut
2023-02-12.abstract: The Wagner paramilitary group said its fighters had seized a village outside Bakhmut, as Moscow’s forces continue a brutal campaign that has nearly encircled the strategic eastern city.

2023-02-12.title: Amnesty in Turkey for Construction Violations Is Scrutinized After Quake
2023-02-12.abstract: Survivors and building experts say poor construction most likely exacerbated the scale of the earthquake’s destruction, as the death toll in Turkey and Syria surpassed 33,000 people.

2023-02-13.title: How China’s ‘Gateway to Europe’ Began to Narrow
2023-02-13.abstract: The Czech Republic, which once courted Beijing in the hope of attracting investment, has soured on China, as have many other countries in Central and Eastern Europe, partly over the war in Ukraine.

2023-02-13.title: Cambodia’s Leader Shuts Independent News Outlet Ahead of Election
2023-02-13.abstract: The leader, Hun Sen, was not satisfied with an apology he received from the outlet after it wrote about his son, a lieutenant general.

2023-02-13.title: ‘Our Losses Were Gigantic’: Life in a Sacrificial Russian Assault Wave
2023-02-13.abstract: Poorly trained Russian soldiers captured by Ukraine describe being used as cannon fodder by commanders throwing waves of bodies into an assault.

2023-02-13.title: China’s Top Airship Scientist Promoted Program to Watch the World From Above
2023-02-13.abstract: Corporate records and media reports reveal an airship scientist at the center of China’s high-altitude balloon program. Companies he has founded were among those targeted by Washington.

2023-02-13.title: China Says U.S. Regularly Sends Balloons Into Its Airspace
2023-02-13.abstract: The claim followed a weekend of aerial theatrics, with Washington and Beijing spotting unidentified flying objects in their airspaces.

2023-02-14.title: Ukraine Bars Aid Workers From Bakhmut as Russia Tightens Its Grip
2023-02-14.abstract: With Moscow’s forces closing in, the besieged city is too dangerous for the volunteers, Ukrainian commanders said, in what could be a prelude to a Ukrainian withdrawal.

2023-02-14.title: Secretive Network Rescues Russia’s Antiwar Dissidents in Nick of Time
2023-02-14.abstract: Hundreds of people facing long prison sentences are being spirited out of the country by groups that arrange daring escapes, with one trip using six different cars over more than 4,000 miles.

2023-02-14.title: Guided Missile Killed U.S. Aid Worker in Ukraine, Video Shows
2023-02-14.abstract: A Times analysis suggests that an intentional strike, not an indiscriminate attack, most likely killed Pete Reed. It is unclear whether the attackers knew he was with a group of aid workers.

2023-02-14.title: China Tries to Depict Furor Over Spy Balloon as Sign of U.S. Decline
2023-02-14.abstract: Ignoring charges of spying, Beijing says Washington’s “overreaction” is a symptom of the country’s broken politics.

2023-02-14.title: Italy’s Hard-Right Leader Vexes Europe by Playing Nice, Mostly
2023-02-14.abstract: Some still fear an authoritarian turn, but Giorgia Meloni has surprised many by showing a pragmatic streak since coming to power. Now Europe is not sure what to do.

2023-02-15.title: New Zealand Declares State of Emergency After Cyclone Gabrielle
2023-02-15.abstract: The cyclone battered most of New Zealand’s North Island and caused severe flooding, landslides and power outages.

2023-02-15.title: As Taliban Settle In, Kabul’s Green Zone Comes Back to Life
2023-02-15.abstract: Walking down its streets a year ago was like wandering into the modern ruins of another empire come and gone from Afghanistan. Now, the Taliban have adopted the former diplomatic enclave as their own.

2023-02-15.title: How Deadly Was China’s Covid Wave?
2023-02-15.abstract: Rough estimates suggest that between 1 and 1.5 million people have died since China ended “zero Covid” — far more than the official count.

2023-02-15.title: In Bleak Russian Cemetery, Sea of Crosses Signals War’s True Toll
2023-02-15.abstract: A dismal, snowy plot near the Black Sea is the final resting place for more and more soldiers from the Wagner mercenary forces, a testament to the huge casualties Russia is suffering in its invasion.

2023-02-15.title: Thousands of Syrian refugees in Turkey rush to border crossings to return home.
2023-02-15.abstract: The Syrian administration of a key border crossing into opposition-held territory in northwestern Syria said that Turkey would allow Syrians to leave the country and then return. Thousands flocked to the border.

2023-02-16.title: Moscow’s Military Capabilities Are in Question After Failed Battle for Ukrainian City
2023-02-16.abstract: A disastrous Russian assault on Vuhledar, viewed as an opening move in an expected spring offensive, has renewed doubts about Moscow’s ability to sustain a large-scale ground assault.

2023-02-16.title: Russia lost about half of its tanks since invading Ukraine, a new report says.
2023-02-16.abstract: A report by the International Institute for Strategic Studies said Russia’s tank and artillery fleets had suffered “significant attrition” in the war.

2023-02-16.title: Syria’s Assad Uses Disaster Diplomacy to Inch Back Onto World Stage
2023-02-16.abstract: “A tragedy for Syrians is a boon for Assad because nobody else wants to manage this mess,” one analyst said of President Bashar al-Assad, who became a global pariah for his regime’s abuses during a civil war.

2023-02-16.title: After Going Gray, a News Anchor Found Herself the Focus of the Story
2023-02-16.abstract: Lisa LaFlamme was dismissed after a decades-long TV career, not long after she stopped dyeing her hair, setting off debates across Canada about sexism, ageism and going gray.

2023-02-16.title: New Zealand, Battered by a Record Storm, Faces a Painful Cleanup
2023-02-16.abstract: Six people were dead and thousands were still unaccounted for after Cyclone Gabrielle lashed the North Island.

2023-02-17.title: New Russian Missile Barrage Hits Ukraine
2023-02-17.abstract: In a blend of the old school and the new, Moscow sent balloons over Ukraine before pummeling it with high-tech weaponry.

2023-02-17.title: How a Tiny NATO Nation Tackled a Big Problem: Arming Ukraine
2023-02-17.abstract: Luxembourg’s challenges in buying arms on the open market underscore the struggle to keep Ukraine supplied until sophisticated Western weapons arrive in the spring.

2023-02-17.title: Why Did a Turkish City Withstand the Quake When Others Crumbled?
2023-02-17.abstract: Erzin survived last week’s 7.8-magnitude quake with no casualties and little damage. The mayor credited his enforcement of building standards, but scientists say it is more likely about geology.

2023-02-17.title: Western officials hope to show unity. But that will be tested as the war drags on.
2023-02-17.abstract: Last year, mere days before Russia’s invasion of Ukraine, dozens of officials from Western nations met in Munich for a security conference in which they discussed the West’s appetite for intervening. Ukraine’s president, Volodymyr Zelensky, attended in person and chastised the West for failing to deter Russian aggression over many years.

2023-02-17.title: Ukraine and Russia swap 202 prisoners, including soldiers who defended Mariupol.
2023-02-17.abstract: Ukraine and Russia exchanged prisoners of war on Thursday, officials from both countries said, including 63 Ukrainian soldiers who had made the last stand at the Azovstal steel plant in the southern city of Mariupol.

2023-02-18.title: He’s Energized Nigeria’s Young Voters. Will They Turn Out for Him?
2023-02-18.abstract: The race is wide open in the presidential election in Nigeria, Africa’s most populous nation and a struggling economic powerhouse. Youth looking to evict the old guard are cheering on Peter Obi, a surprise third-party candidate.

2023-02-18.title: Trading Books for a Rifle: The Teacher Who Volunteered in Ukraine
2023-02-18.abstract: When Russia invaded, recruiting officers asked no questions of the Ukrainian teacher. They handed her a rifle and 120 bullets, and assigned her to a unit expecting to fight in urban combat if the Russian Army broke into Kyiv.

2023-02-18.title: Caves, Tents and Shipping Containers: Quake Survivors’ Desperate Hunt for Shelter
2023-02-18.abstract: For now, the Turkish government is relying on short-term solutions to house an estimated one million who are crowding into gyms, hotels and dormitories. A cruise ship is set to arrive to house thousands.

2023-02-18.title: China’s Balloons Draw Attention to an Overlooked Canada-U.S. Partnership
2023-02-18.abstract: For more than 60 years the two countries have shared responsibility for protecting North American airspace through the joint command known as NORAD.

2023-02-18.title: A top Chinese official calls the U.S. balloon response ‘hysterical’ and says the war in Ukraine must not continue.
2023-02-18.abstract: Wang Yi spoke at the Munich Security Conference as suspense mounted over whether he might meet on the sidelines with Secretary of State Antony J. Blinken.

2023-02-19.title: Syrian State Media Reports a Deadly Strike in Damascus
2023-02-19.abstract: Attributed in Syria to Israel, the strike appeared to be the latest episode of a long-running conflict between Israel and Iranian proxies.

2023-02-19.title: ‘Brainwashing a Generation’: British Schools Combat Andrew Tate’s Views
2023-02-19.abstract: Alarmed by an online personality’s popularity among their students, educators are mobilizing to combat the sexism he promotes.

2023-02-19.title: A High-Maintenance Relationship for 637 Years, but Milan’s Duomo Is Still Adored
2023-02-19.abstract: The care for Milan’s cathedral has been nonstop since 1386, but despite the constant need for refurbishment, the beloved landmark’s hold on the city is unbreakable.

2023-02-19.title: Despised Dictator’s ‘Scary’ Shrine Becomes a Bet on Albania’s Future
2023-02-19.abstract: An enormous pyramid was long a reminder of a brutal regime and decades of disappointments. Now, it’s a symbol of a city aspiring to be the high-tech “Tel Aviv of the Balkans.”

2023-02-19.title: Despite Help for Venezuelans in South America, Many Are Still Heading North
2023-02-19.abstract: Colombia, with U.S. help, is providing temporary visas to Venezuelan migrants. Still, financial struggles are leading many to leave for the United States.

2023-02-20.title: Despite War, Ukrainian Dance Group Returns for the ‘America’s Got Talent’ Finale
2023-02-20.abstract: Scattered around the globe by the conflict, the group pulled together enough performers and came up with a winning routine in five days.

2023-02-20.title: A Nobel Peace Prize Winner Sees Perils in the West’s Focus on Ukraine War
2023-02-20.abstract: Juan Manuel Santos, a former president of Colombia, said the Ukraine war was “sucking all the energy” away from fighting other challenges, including climate change.

2023-02-20.title: They Were Married. They Shared a Trench. They Died in It Together.
2023-02-20.abstract: Taras and Olha Melster signed up to help the war effort. Like many other urban professionals in Ukraine, they never expected to be sent to the front line.

2023-02-20.title: China Says U.S. Is ‘Not Qualified’ to Issue Orders on Arms
2023-02-20.abstract: Beijing is trying to preserve ties with Moscow while asserting it is a neutral observer trying only to coax Russia and Ukraine into peace talks.

2023-02-20.title: Israeli Lawmakers Move to Rein In Judges as Protests Rock Jerusalem
2023-02-20.abstract: Two contentious bills were approved in an initial vote in Parliament on Monday, including one that would reduce the power of the Supreme Court.

2023-02-21.title: Are North Korea’s Nuclear Weapons Tests as Safe as the Country Claims?
2023-02-21.abstract: The country has conducted six underground nuclear tests, and a seventh may be on the way. Rights groups fear their environmental and health consequences.

2023-02-21.title: Victim or Criminal? A U.S. Navy Officer’s Imprisonment in Japan.
2023-02-21.abstract: Lt. Ridge Alkonis was sentenced to three years after causing a deadly traffic accident in 2021. The case has irritated U.S. relations with a crucial military ally.

2023-02-21.title: ‘Equality of Injustice for All’: Saudi Arabia Expands Crackdown on Dissent
2023-02-21.abstract: The kingdom’s courts are meting out harsher punishments than ever to citizens who criticize the government, with prosecutions built on Twitter posts ending in prison sentences of 15 to 45 years.

2023-02-21.title: In Northern Ukraine, a Different Sort of War Game
2023-02-21.abstract: Russian forces are shelling border areas there to tie up thousands of Ukrainian troops that might otherwise defend against attacks farther south.

2023-02-21.title: Remembering the Lives Saved Drives Rescuers in Turkey to Push On
2023-02-21.abstract: In the third week of a grueling effort, emergency workers battle exhaustion as the hope of finding more earthquake survivors dwindles. A powerful new quake has complicated their task.

2023-02-22.title: Biden Accuses Putin of Atrocities and Urges World to Rebuke Him
2023-02-22.abstract: In sharply opposed speeches, President Biden said Vladimir V. Putin bore sole responsibility for the war, while Mr. Putin said Russia had invaded in self-defense. But they agreed the war would not end soon.

2023-02-22.title: South Korea Inches Toward Same-Sex Equality, but Broader Bill Is Stalled
2023-02-22.abstract: Christian conservatives in the country have campaigned tirelessly for decades to prevent legislation that would offer protections to L.G.B.T.Q. people.

2023-02-22.title: Biden’s Meeting With Eastern European Leaders Will Cap His Trip
2023-02-22.abstract: The nine countries along NATO’s eastern flank are the likeliest targets of another Russian invasion.

2023-02-22.title: U.K. Court Upholds Ruling Stripping Shamima Begum’s Citizenship
2023-02-22.abstract: Ms. Begum was one of three teenage girls from London who traveled to Syria in 2015 to join the Islamic State.

2023-02-22.title: How Poland, Long Leery of Foreigners, Opened Up to Ukrainians
2023-02-22.abstract: The country, once one of the world’s most ethnically and culturally homogeneous, has accommodated far more refugees from neighboring Ukraine than any other nation.

2023-02-23.title: Mexico Hobbles Election Agency That Helped End One-Party Rule
2023-02-23.abstract: The changes come ahead of a presidential election next year and are part of a pattern of challenges to democratic institutions across the Western Hemisphere.

2023-02-23.title: Putin and Biden Shore Up Alliances in Dueling Appearances
2023-02-23.abstract: On the surface, it looked like a reversion to the Cold War era. The reality was even more complicated.

2023-02-23.title: A National Treasure on the Rise Is Hit With a Doping Allegation
2023-02-23.abstract: A star Olympic runner from Australia was suspended over a positive drug test of a kind that critics have called alarmingly open to human error.

2023-02-23.title: More Than 50 Missing After Coal Mine Collapses in Northern China
2023-02-23.abstract: By Thursday, four deaths had been confirmed as rescuers worked to save miners at the site in Inner Mongolia.

2023-02-23.title: Another Brexit Deal Is Another Gamble for Another Prime Minister
2023-02-23.abstract: Ever since Britain voted to leave the European Union, the question of Northern Ireland’s border has bedeviled prime ministers. Rishi Sunak’s effort to broker a deal is already under threat.

2023-02-24.title: Lethal Threat or Tolerable Risk? Ukrainians Must Judge Constantly.
2023-02-24.abstract: After a year of war, people in Ukraine have become adept at telling which threats are probably not deadly, leaving room for a little enjoyment, and even hope.

2023-02-24.title: Shattered Homes and Lurking Disease: Quake Hardships Pile Up
2023-02-24.abstract: Hundreds of thousands are sheltering in tents, breathing air thick with pollutants unleashed from tombs of rubble, fearful that a new disaster could strike at any moment.

2023-02-24.title: Yellen Confronts Russian Officials at G20 Meeting
2023-02-24.abstract: In a private session, Treasury Secretary Janet L. Yellen said the Russians were “complicit in Putin’s atrocities,” according to remarks shared by a U.S. official.

2023-02-24.title: That Mystery Orb on a Japanese Beach? It Was Just a Buoy. (Sorry.)
2023-02-24.abstract: Experts said the sphere that washed ashore, leading to speculation about espionage, was nothing special. “Maybe everybody is paranoid because of balloons,” one said.

2023-02-24.title: Ahead of Crucial Election, Security Crises and Kidnappings Plague Nigeria
2023-02-24.abstract: Nigerian voters say that insecurity is the most important issue in this week’s presidential election. One man who was kidnapped said, “You can only survive on your own in Nigeria.”

2023-02-25.title: Robert Hébras, Last Survivor of a 1944 Massacre in France, Dies at 97
2023-02-25.abstract: Dead bodies shielded him as the Nazis killed 643 people in Oradour-sur-Glane. He spent his life keeping the memory of the slaughter alive.

2023-02-25.title: Protests and Defiance Mark a Year of Russia’s War on Ukraine
2023-02-25.abstract: On the anniversary of the invasion, Volodymyr Zelensky held a marathon news conference and vowed victory if Ukraine’s allies remained united like a fist.

2023-02-25.title: After 8 Lost Years, a Wide-Open Election in the Giant of Africa
2023-02-25.abstract: In Nigeria’s presidential election on Saturday, voters were desperate to elect a leader who can unleash their youthful country’s potential and chart a new course after years of dashed hopes.

2023-02-25.title: Their Hair Long and Flowing or in Ponytails, Women in Iran Flaunt Their Locks
2023-02-25.abstract: Defiant resistance to Iran’s mandatory hijab law has exploded across the country after nationwide protests that erupted last year.

2023-02-25.title: For the First Time in 52 Years, There’s No Skating Through the Capital
2023-02-25.abstract: A winter of gyrating temperatures and rainfalls caused officials to give up on the “world’s largest skating rink.”

2023-02-26.title: After Model’s Gruesome Killing, Suspicion Falls on Ex-Husband and His Family
2023-02-26.abstract: Abby Choi, known for her glamorous Instagram posts, was reported missing on Wednesday. On Friday, the Hong Kong police found her remains, dismembered and incomplete.

2023-02-26.title: Desperate for Babies, China Races to Undo an Era of Birth Limits. Is It Too Late?
2023-02-26.abstract: A number of new incentives encouraging people to have children highlight the challenges China faces in trying to boost its declining birthrate.

2023-02-26.title: War in Ukraine Has Changed Europe Forever
2023-02-26.abstract: No event has transformed the continent more profoundly since the end of the Cold War, and there is no going back now.

2023-02-26.title: Deadly Russian Artillery Strikes Reported in Ukraine’s East and South
2023-02-26.abstract: Officials said at least five civilians were killed in the attacks, as President Vladimir V. Putin repeated his claim that the war is part of a U.S.-led effort to destroy Russia.

2023-02-26.title: U.K. Nears Deal on Northern Ireland Trade, a Brexit Sore Point
2023-02-26.abstract: Prime Minister Rishi Sunak is expected to meet on Monday in Britain with the president of the European Commission, Ursula von der Leyen, to work out the final details.

2023-02-27.title: China Dismisses Latest Claim That Lab Leak Likely Caused Covid
2023-02-27.abstract: The response came after the Energy Department concluded with “low confidence” that the coronavirus emerged from a lab in Wuhan, China.

2023-02-27.title: Russia’s New Offensive Sends Conscripts Into the Teeth of Ukraine’s Lines
2023-02-27.abstract: A month into the campaign, Russian forces have barely budged despite forays by small units in fields, pine forests and ruined towns.

2023-02-27.title: Dying Children and Frozen Flocks in Afghanistan’s Bitter Winter of Crisis
2023-02-27.abstract: Hundreds have died in plunging temperatures, and malnutrition has been rampant as the Taliban government’s ban on female workers has hampered international aid.

2023-02-27.title: Revenge Attacks After Killing of Israeli Settlers Leave West Bank in Turmoil
2023-02-27.abstract: Settlers burned and vandalized hundreds of cars and homes in four Palestinian villages, according to Israeli rights groups and Palestinian officials, and the area braced for more violence.

2023-02-27.title: New Earthquake Strikes Turkey, Rattling an Already Devastated Region
2023-02-27.abstract: The latest temblor, measuring 5.2 in magnitude, shook parts of the same area stricken by a far stronger quake on Feb. 6 that killed more than 50,000 people in the country and in neighboring Syria.

2023-02-28.title: Hong Kong, One of World’s Last Holdouts, Ends Its Mask Mandate
2023-02-28.abstract: As of Wednesday, the city will no longer require people to wear masks indoors, outdoors or on public transportation.

2023-02-28.title: Burger King Is Hungry Jack’s in Australia. What Will Wendy’s Be?
2023-02-28.abstract: The American burger chain plans to enter the Australian market. The problem? There’s already a fast food company down under with the same name.

2023-02-28.title: Scrounging for Tanks for Ukraine, Europe’s Armies Come Up Short
2023-02-28.abstract: The struggle to deliver on promises to provide Leopard 2 tanks for use against Russian forces has exposed just how unprepared European militaries are.

2023-02-28.title: China Moves to Erase the Vestiges of ‘Zero Covid’ to Deter Dissent
2023-02-28.abstract: The ruling Communist Party is waging an ambitious propaganda campaign to rewrite the public’s memory of its handling of the pandemic, which included some of the harshest restrictions in the world.

2023-02-28.title: Sunak Hopes to Move Past Brexit, at Long Last, With E.U. Deal
2023-02-28.abstract: In addition to removing an obstacle to London-Brussels relations, the Northern Ireland trade deal could remove Brexit from the center of British politics after seven divisive years.
